Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Dec-06-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS ALEXANDRE MATTOS, AND CARLA MATTOS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Granted in part. As to requests pertaining to Plaintiff's vehicle and Defendant's policies, Defendant shall provide amended verified Code-complaint responses and any responsive documents within 30 days; specifically, if Defendant withholds information pursuant to an objection, then it shall identify with particularity the specific document or evidence demanded as to which the objection is made and set forth the specific ground for objection. CCP 2031.240(b); Standon Co., Inc. v Superior Court (1990) 225 CA3d 898, 901 (objections constitute implicit refusals to produce). Separately, the requests for information pertaining to other vehicles, as drafted, are overbroad but also seek information that is potentially relevant to Plaintiff's claims, according to the cases cited by Plaintiff. Counsel for the parties shall meet and confer promptly in good faith, preferably remotely or in person this time, regarding the scope of the requests and, if necessary, to narrow the issues in dispute. No sanctions as to this motion but sanctions may be awarded against either side if the motion is renewed.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and email it to Judge Pro Tem Aaron Minnis at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com .

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (525/JPT)
